title: Parsing: the merit of strictly typed JSON
author: Max Duval
summary: Dealing with content that keeps changing can lead to bugs on users’ devices that are hard to reproduce. By precisely describing the shape of the content using types and ensuring it matches, exceptions can be avoided and users shown helpful messages when errors occur. This post will illustrate how this can be achieved with standard web technologies. The programming language…

title: When security matters: working with Qubes OS at the Guardian
author: Philip McMahon
summary: The latest version of the whistleblowing platform SecureDrop runs on the Qubes operating system. At the Guardian we used the Salt management engine to set up a Qubes environment where journalists could safely interrogate sensitive documents. title: Making Typerighter work harder
author: Rhys Mills
summary: How we improved Typerighter, the Guardian’s style guide checker, to provide more value for the Guardian and its readers Minimising typos is important to a newspaper.
